VIA CHRISTI VILLAGE HAYS KS LLC

Overview

Address
2225 CANTERBURY DR, HAYS, KS 67601
Phone
7856283241
Certified beds
96
Avg daily residents
87 (90% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid

2007-07-09
Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.57 3.43 5.14 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.98 0.60 1.11 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.57 0.76 0.64 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 3.01 2.07 3.39 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.56 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.25
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.58
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.79
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
90.2%
annual
RN turnover
64.7%
annual
Administrators departed
2
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.22
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.88
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-07-23 20 20 0 140 1 140
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-11-30 17 14 7 116 1 116
Total weighted health score
134.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (47)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

45 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. E 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. F 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-07-23 2025-09-16
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-10-29 2024-11-18
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. E 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0638 Assure that each resident’s assessment is updated at least once every 3 months. E 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. F 2023-11-30 2024-01-05
0678 Provide basic life support, including CPR, prior to the arrival of emergency medical personnel , subject to physician orders and the resident’s advance directives. K 2023-08-17 2023-07-28
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2023-03-28 2023-04-30
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2022-06-07 2022-08-22
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2022-06-07 2022-08-22
0661 Ensure necessary information is communicated to the resident, and receiving health care provider at the time of a planned discharge. D 2022-06-07 2022-08-22
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2022-06-07 2022-08-22
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2022-06-07 2022-08-22
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2022-06-07 2022-08-22
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2022-06-07 2022-08-22
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2022-06-07 2022-08-22
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2022-06-07 2022-08-22
